finish_answer:
## Biography of Samuel ÃghalÃ© Oboh

**Samuel ÃghalÃ© Oboh** (born March 27, 1971, in Lagos, Nigeria) is a prominent Canadian architect, leader, and diplomat. He is widely recognized as a trailblazer in the field of architecture, becoming the first Canadian of African descent to serve as President of the Royal Architectural Institute of Canada (RAIC) in 2015. He is currently associated with Ensight+ Architecture and serves as the Consul for the Republic of Botswana in Canada.

### Early Life and Education
Oboh was born in Lagos, Nigeria, the third of six children. His father, Johnson Oboh, was a mechanical engineering technician, and the family lived in various parts of Nigeria including Lagos, Maiduguri, and Yola. Oboh graduated at the top of his high school class in 1987 at just 16 years old and was accepted to Bendel State University (now Ambrose Alli University) to study architecture, earning his B.Sc. with Upper Second-Class Honours in 1992. He then completed a Master of Science in Architecture at Ahmadu Bello University, Zaria, in 1996. During his graduate studies, he attended the Africa 2000 Conference on Architecture at the University of Cape Town, which proved pivotal for his career. He later earned a second master's degree â a Master of Arts in Communication & Technology from the University of Alberta in 2010, where he received the Herbert Marshall McLuhan Graduate Student Award.

### Career
After completing his national youth service in Katsina, Nigeria, Oboh joined F&A Services as a design architect, working on the refurbishment of the historic Federal Palace Hotel in Lagos. In 1998, he became an Adjunct Lecturer at ML Sultan Technikon (now Durban University of Technology) in South Africa, teaching African Architecture. He then joined FMA Architects Southern Africa in Gaborone, Botswana, where he served as Resident Architect on major projects including the **Botswana Police College** in Otse and the **International Law Enforcement Academy** (the result of a bilateral agreement between Botswana and the United States). He also served as Resident Architect on the **$150-million Maun District Hospital** in northern Botswana.

Oboh emigrated to Canada in December 2003 and joined **IBI Group Architects**, where he obtained his LEED Accredited Professional designation. He played a key sustainability role on the Edmonton Police Service Southeast Division Station â the first LEED Gold-certified police station in North America. He also served as IBI Group's Project Architect for the **$118-million City of Red Deer Civic Yards**.

In 2007, Oboh joined **Kasian Architecture** as an associate and became a Principal in 2016. As Lead Architect for the **Alberta Legislature Centre Redevelopment Master Plan**, he guided the creative vision for Alberta's most significant heritage site. From 2010 to 2016, he also served with the Department of Public Works and Government Services Canada as Regional Manager of the Architecture and Engineering Centre of Expertise in the Western Region, overseeing a team of over 50 professionals.

Oboh later served as **Vice President â Architecture at AECOM Canada Architects Ltd**, a Fortune 500 company. In 2021, at the Rio General Assembly, he was elected **Vice President for Region 3 of the International Union of Architects (UIA)**, a Paris-based body recognized by the United Nations.

He is licensed as an architect in multiple jurisdictions including the U.S. state of Texas and the Canadian provinces of Alberta, Ontario, and British Columbia, and was previously licensed in Botswana and South Africa. He has held adjunct lecturer/visiting critic roles at the University of Calgary, University of Toronto, Carleton University, University of Pretoria, and Durban University of Technology.

### Awards and Honors
Oboh's extensive list of awards and recognitions includes:
- **2024 King Charles III Coronation Medal** (for contributions to Architecture)
- **2024 BOND Awards** (Trailblazer in Architecture), Toronto
- **2023 Tom Sutherland Architecture Award** in Canada
- **2023 Honorary Fellow**, Nigerian Institute of Architects (Hon. FNIA)
- **2022 Honorary Award**, Colegio de Arquitectos de Bolivia
- **2018 Distinguished Fellow of the American Institute of Architects (FAIA)** â only about 3% of architects hold this distinction
- **2015 AIA Presidential Medal** and **Honorary Member of the Royal Australian Institute of Architects**
- Named one of **Canada's 150 Extraordinary Canadians** and one of **Alberta's 50 Most Influential People**

### Notable Projects
- Alberta Legislature Centre Redevelopment Project (Edmonton)
- Red Deer Civic Yards
- Villa Caritas
- International Law Enforcement Academy (Gaborone, Botswana)
- Botswana Police College (Otse, Botswana)
- Maun District Hospital (Botswana)
- Diplomacy by Design: A Design Excellence Framework for Canadian Embassies and Missions Abroad
- Royal Alberta Museum of History Development (Edmonton)
- Alberta at the Smithsonian Folklife Festival (Washington, DC)

### Personal Life
Samuel Oboh is married and has three children: Noora, Fego, and Oreva. His parents are Eunice and Johnson Oboh. Since late 2015, he has also served in a diplomatic capacity as the highest-ranking representative and Consul for the Republic of Botswana in Canada.

[Source: Wikipedia â "Samuel Oboh"]
